page: Amazon Web Services m8gd.large is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. This server features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at 2.8 GHz, with a cache configuration of 64 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 36 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. Single-core performance is generally average, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1707.39 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1810.0. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is a notable strength, ranking in the top 25% with a score of 14.13 GB/sec. However, multi-core and multi-threaded capabilities are limited by the dual-core configuration, resulting in weak multi-core scores across stress-ng (3403.17) and Geekbench (3234.0). Memory performance is also split, showing average read-only bandwidth for large blocks (56.19 GB/sec) but weak write bandwidth (22.47 GB/sec) and poor Passmark CPU scores.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8gd.large offers a balanced entry-point for ARM64-based workloads requiring local high-speed NVMe storage without the overhead of larger instance sizes. The inclusion of the 118 GB local instance store provides a resource-dense option for temporary caching or scratch space. Given its hardware profile and average database operations benchmark (3023.03), this instance is suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, basic database operations, single-threaded processing tasks, and static web serving. It is less suitable for multi-threaded compute-intensive tasks or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.large is a general-purpose, ARM64-based instance powered by a 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores (2 vCPUs), 8.0 GB of system memory, and a bundled 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, highlighted by a strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 14.13 GB/sec, though multi-core performance remains limited due to the dual-core architecture. The instance offers a cost-efficient profile for resource-dense workloads that benefit from local high-speed storage. It is best suited for lightweight database operations, basic web serving, and single-threaded processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.large is an ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store, offering balanced single-core performance for lightweight database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gd.large is a 2-vCPU ARM64 instance with 8 GB RAM and 118 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated physical cores and integrated NVMe SSD storage for balanced, localized workloads.

Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and a 118 GB NVMe SSD instance store
Delivers strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance of 14.13 GB/sec

Best suited for lightweight database operations, static web serving, and single-threaded tasks
